assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following diagrams best represents the synthesis of leading strands and Okazaki fragments in a replication bubble?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: All of the diagrams depict both long and short arrows, which represent growing leading strands and Okazaki fragments, respectively. However, the diagrams differ in how these arrows are oriented relative to the template strands. In the correct diagram, all of the arrows will point in the direction of DNA synthesis, as is convention.

DNA synthesis always occurs antiparallel to the template DNA and in the 5’ to 3’ direction. So, in the correct diagram, each arrow will point towards the 5’ end of its template strand:

[[☃ image 1]]

The correct diagram is

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A replication bubble is shown bulging between double stranded D N A. From left to right the top D N A strand runs 5 prime to 3 prime and the bottom D N A strand runs 3 prime to 5 prime. The top strand is paired with a leading strand on the left and a series of 3 fragments on the right, all of which are growing to the left. The bottom strand is paired with a series of 3 fragments on the left and a leading strand on the right, all of which are growing to the right. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dd16f0e3c838e1a9a181269a27f82ccd.png)

![A replication bubble is shown bulging between double stranded D N A. From left to right the top D N A strand runs 5 prime to 3 prime and the bottom D N A strand runs 3 prime to 5 prime. The top strand is paired with a leading strand on the left, growing to the right, and a series of 3 fragments on the right, growing to the left. The bottom strand is paired with a series of 3 fragments on the left, growing to the right, and a leading strand on the right, growing to the left. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8e4bdd17617a31366ca9c25475c54d52.png)

![A replication bubble is shown bulging between double stranded D N A. From left to right the top D N A strand runs 5 prime to 3 prime and the bottom D N A strand runs 3 prime to 5 prime. The top strand is paired with a leading strand on the left, growing to the left, and a series of 3 fragments on the right, growing to the right. The bottom strand is paired with a series of 3 fragments on the left, growing to the left, and a leading strand on the right, growing to the right. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3c333330dec9d26e95e680e77638789b.png)

![A replication bubble is shown bulging between double stranded D N A. From left to right the top D N A strand runs 5 prime to 3 prime and the bottom D N A strand runs 3 prime to 5 prime. The top strand is paired with a leading strand on the left and a series of 3 fragments on the right, all of which are growing to the right. The bottom strand is paired with a series of 3 fragments on the left and a leading strand on the right, all of which are growing to the left. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2e45190e516350217458560571778c24.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following diagrams best represents leading strand and Okazaki fragment synthesis at a replication fork?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: All of the diagrams depict one long arrow, which represents a growing leading strand, and two short arrows, which represent growing Okazaki fragments. However, the diagrams differ in how these arrows are oriented relative to the template strands. In the correct diagram, all of the arrows will point in the direction of DNA synthesis, as is convention.

DNA synthesis always occurs antiparallel to the template DNA and in the 5’ to 3’ direction. So, in the correct diagram, each arrow will point towards the 5’ end of its template strand:

[[☃ image 1]]

The correct diagram is

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A diagram showing D N A replication. Separated double strand D N A is shown in black and is connected on the left hand side. The top black strand runs 5 prime on the left to 3 prime on the right and is attached to a red strand that is growing from right to left. The bottom black strand runs 3 prime on the left to 5 prime on the right and is attached to a blue pair of fragments growing left to right.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c3b2e29a7b01dc84cbaf032040653ce2.png)

![A diagram showing D N A replication. Separated double strand D N A is shown in black and is connected on the left hand side. The top black strand runs 5 prime on the left to 3 prime on the right and is attached to a red strand that is growing from right to left. The bottom black strand runs 3 prime on the left to 5 prime on the right and is attached to a blue pair of fragments growing right to left.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d642a7fe75674c96f646d9afcee359aa.png)

![A diagram showing D N A replication. Separated double strand D N A is shown in black and is connected on the left hand side. The top black strand runs 5 prime on the left to 3 prime on the right and is attached to a red strand that is growing from left to right. The bottom black strand runs 3 prime on the left to 5 prime on the right and is attached to a blue pair of fragments growing left to right.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f1cc1fb9454d28859544dd9048dec755.png)

![A diagram showing D N A replication. Separated double strand D N A is shown in black and is connected on the left hand side. The top black strand runs 5 prime on the left to 3 prime on the right and is attached to a red strand that is growing from left to right. The bottom black strand runs 3 prime on the left to 5 prime on the right and is attached to a blue pair of fragments growing right to left.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/afd119eb877cfce8744dc872417a3393.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following models most accurately represents the process of DNA replication over two cell divisions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: During replication, each strand of a DNA molecule serves as a template for a new, complementary strand.
This means that each daughter DNA molecule is made up of one parental and one newly-synthesized strand.

So, the diagram that most accurately represents the process of DNA replication is

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![Blue duplex D N A is replicated into two blue/red duplexes. Each blue/red duplex is further replicated into one blue/black duplex and one black/red duplex. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb2c84ca75231cdf4ba623c3f2bb4de3.svg)

![Blue duplex D N A is replicated into one red duplex and one blue duplex. The red duplex is further replicated into one red duplex and one black duplex. The blue duplex is further replicated into one black duplex and one blue duplex.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4bc12a64979432577e2818bfa6474f5e.svg)

![Blue duplex D N A is replicated into one blue/red duplex and one blue duplex. The blue/red duplex is further replicated into one blue/black duplex and one blue/red duplex. The blue duplex is further replicated into one blue/black duplex and one blue duplex.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/008a4a719561a879077c26487e4996f7.svg)

![Blue duplex D N A is replicated into two duplexes. Each of these duplexes contains a strand that is half red and half blue. The opposite strand is also half blue and half red, but in the opposite orientation so that blue bairs pairs with red. Each of these duplexes is further replicated into a duplex containing a mix of blue, red, and black. Each of these duplexes contains a strand that is half red and half blue partnered with a strand that is half red and half black, with the red paired with black in part of the duplex and red paired with blue in the other part. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca709e3a6016c8149732451ccb1c90aa.svg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which of the following diagrams of DNA replication are the enzymes correctly labeled?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Each diagram shows the following four enzymes: DNA polymerase, ligase, helicase, and topoisomerase. In the correctly labeled diagram, the depiction of each enzyme will match its function.

Let’s start by considering the two enzymes on the left side of each diagram:

[[☃ image 1]]

According to the labels on the diagrams, one of these enzymes is helicase and one is topoisomerase. The enzyme on the right is shown interacting with the DNA just upstream of the replication fork, so this enzyme is likely helicase, which helps separate the two strands of a DNA template. In contrast, the enzyme on the left is shown interacting with the DNA further upstream, so this enzyme is likely topoisomerase, which helps relax and unwind supercoiled DNA.

Next, let’s consider the enzymes on the right side of each diagram:

[[☃ image 1]]

According to the labels on the diagrams, these enzymes represent DNA polymerase and ligase. The enzyme on the right is shown interacting with the ends of two DNA fragments, so this enzyme is likely ligase, which joins Okazaki fragments together. In contrast, the enzymes on the left are shown interacting with the growing ends of new DNA strands, so these enzymes are likely DNA polymerases, which add nucleotides to new strands during DNA replication.

So, the correctly labeled diagram is

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A diagram showing D N A replication. Double stranded D N A on the left is associated with a circle labeled topoisomerase. The replication fork is associated with a crescent labeled helicase. The top D N A strand is paired with a strand growing toward the replication fork and is associated with an oval labeled D N A polymerase. The bottom D N A strand is paired with 3 fragments. The fragment nearest the replication fork is growing away from the fork and is associated with an oval labeled D N A polymerase. The gap between the other 2 fragments is associated with a rectangle labeled ligase.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1d130dddb0acfb2dd1faaec4d8d7c11f.png)

![A diagram showing D N A replication. Double stranded D N A on the left is associated with a circle labeled helicase. The replication fork is associated with a crescent labeled topoisomerase. The top D N A strand is paired with a strand growing toward the replication fork and is associated with an oval labeled D N A polymerase. The bottom D N A strand is paired with 3 fragments. The fragment nearest the replication fork is growing away from the fork and is associated with an oval labeled D N A polymerase. The gap between the other 2 fragments is associated with a rectangle labeled ligase.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/de018e89914828e1872e9753429640ff.png)

![A diagram showing D N A replication. Double stranded D N A on the left is associated with a circle labeled topoisomerase. The replication fork is associated with a crescent labeled helicase. The top D N A strand is paired with a strand growing toward the replication fork and is associated with an oval labeled ligase. The bottom D N A strand is paired with 3 fragments. The fragment nearest the replication fork is growing away from the fork and is associated with an oval labeled ligase. The gap between the other 2 fragments is associated with a rectangle labeled D N A polymerase.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1a0d928c51e4cd72755a6ad53740028.png)

![A diagram showing D N A replication. Double stranded D N A on the left is associated with a circle labeled helicase. The replication fork is associated with a crescent labeled topoisomerase. The top D N A strand is paired with a strand growing toward the replication fork and is associated with an oval labeled ligase. The bottom D N A strand is paired with 3 fragments. The fragment nearest the replication fork is growing away from the fork and is associated with an oval labeled ligase. The gap between the other 2 fragments is associated with a rectangle labeled D N A polymerase.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d02497f748dbde850742934f02a4baa.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
The following symbols represent enzymes involved in DNA replication.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Given the symbols above, which of the following diagrams most accurately depicts the process of DNA replication ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Each diagram includes symbols for DNA polymerase, ligase, helicase, and topoisomerase. In the diagram that most accurately represents DNA replication, these symbols will be placed in a way that corresponds to the function of each enzyme.

Let’s start by considering the left side of each diagram where we find the symbols for helicase and topoisomerase. Helicase helps separate the two strands of a DNA template, so its symbol should be placed just at the opening of the replication fork. In contrast, topoisomerase helps relax and unwind supercoiled DNA, so its symbol should be placed on the double-stranded DNA upstream from helicase.

Taken together, this means that the left side of the correct diagram will look like this:

[[☃ image 1]]

Next, let’s consider the right side of each diagram where we find the symbols for DNA polymerase and ligase. DNA polymerase adds nucleotides to new DNA strands during replication, so its symbol should be placed at the growing ends of the new DNA strands (which are represented by arrows in the diagrams). In contrast, ligase joins Okazaki fragments together, so its symbol should be placed between the two DNA fragments.

Taken together, this means that the right side of the correct diagram will look like this:

[[☃ image 1]]

So, the correct diagram is

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A key showing the which shapes represent which enzymes. Circle equals topoisomerase. Crescent equals helicase. Triangle equals D N A polymerase. Rectangle equals ligase.

choices.content: ![A diagram showing D N A replication. Double stranded D N A on the left is associated with a circle. The replication fork is associated with a crescent. The top D N A strand is paired with a strand growing toward the replication fork and is associated with a triangle. The bottom D N A strand is paired with 3 fragments. The fragment nearest the replication fork is growing away from the fork and is associated with a triangle. The gap between the other 2 fragments is associated with a rectangle.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/826b6e952c8ba79b35fb3e5566dbed4b.png)

![A diagram showing D N A replication. Double stranded D N A on the left is associated with a crescent. The replication fork is associated with a circle. The top D N A strand is paired with a strand growing toward the replication fork and is associated with a triangle. The bottom D N A strand is paired with 3 fragments. The fragment nearest the replication fork is growing away from the fork and is associated with a triangle. The gap between the other 2 fragments is associated with a rectangle.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4cd41327d7e9433d8d5c63617098b421.png)

![A diagram showing D N A replication. Double stranded D N A on the left is associated with a circle. The replication fork is associated with a crescent. The top D N A strand is paired with a strand growing toward the replication fork and is associated with a rectangle. The bottom D N A strand is paired with 3 fragments. The fragment nearest the replication fork is growing away from the fork and is associated with a rectangle. The gap between the other 2 fragments is associated with a triangle.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6e9cb8b02a1a2259791a99cd97138e0c.png)

![A diagram showing D N A replication. Double stranded D N A on the left is associated with a crescent. The replication fork is associated with a circle. The top D N A strand is paired with a strand growing toward the replication fork and is associated with a rectangle. The bottom D N A strand is paired with 3 fragments. The fragment nearest the replication fork is growing away from the fork and is associated with a rectangle. The gap between the other 2 fragments is associated with a triangle.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6bef99f26d0850ccb3b84451605c21c3.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
**In DNA replication, which of the following events happens during both leading *and* lagging strand synthesis?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: RNA primers help initiate DNA synthesis.
DNA polymerase synthesizes a single, continuous strand of DNA.
Topoisomerase separates complementary strands of DNA.
Ligase joins together multiple Okazaki fragments.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In DNA replication, which of the following events happens during leading strand synthesis but *not* during lagging strand synthesis?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: DNA polymerase synthesizes a single, continuous strand of DNA.
RNA primers help initiate DNA synthesis.
Ligase joins together multiple Okazaki fragments.
Helicase helps relax supercoiled DNA upstream of the replication fork.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true regarding DNA replication?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: During lagging strand synthesis, ligase joins together multiple Okazaki fragments.
During leading strand synthesis, a DNA primer helps initiate DNA synthesis.
During leading strand synthesis, DNA polymerase synthesizes short, discontinuous strands of DNA.
During lagging strand synthesis, topoisomerase separates complementary strands of DNA.
